name: Construction & Site Management
description: Construction and site management process readiness assessment
persona: You are a senior construction consultant with 20+ years of experience in project delivery, contract management, and site operations. You specialize in EPC projects, NEC/FIDIC contracts, and digital transformation in construction.

broadAreas.name: Project-to-Delivery
broadAreas.description: Project management, site operations, workforce, and equipment management

subAreas.name: Project Management
subAreas.description: Project planning, scheduling, milestone tracking, EVM
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about project planning methodologies, scheduling tools, milestone tracking, earned value management, and how project performance is monitored and reported.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Projects managed with paper-based plans and ad-hoc scheduling; no formal baseline or progress tracking
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic Gantt charts produced in spreadsheets or simple tools; milestones tracked manually with periodic reporting
benchmarks.maturity_3: Dedicated project management software used for scheduling; baseline vs actual tracked with some earned value metrics
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated project controls platform with live EVM dashboards, automated lookaheads, and cross-project visibility
benchmarks.maturity_5: Fully digital project management with AI-driven schedule risk analysis, real-time progress updates from site, and predictive completion forecasting

subAreas.name: Site Operations
subAreas.description: Daily site management, progress monitoring, QA/QC, handover
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about daily site reporting, progress measurement methods, quality assurance and control processes, inspection and test plans, punch list management, and handover procedures.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Site diaries and daily reports completed on paper; QA/QC checks informal and undocumented; handover relies on physical dossiers
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic digital forms for site reporting; inspection checklists used but stored in spreadsheets; handover documentation manually compiled
benchmarks.maturity_3: Mobile-based site reporting tools deployed; QA/QC integrated with document management; punch lists tracked digitally with owner assignments
benchmarks.maturity_4: Real-time site progress captured via mobile and IoT sensors; QA/QC linked to work packages; handover managed through structured close-out workflows
benchmarks.maturity_5: Drone surveys, BIM model updates, and wearable technology provide continuous site visibility; AI-assisted defect detection; fully digital handover with as-built BIM and O&M manuals

subAreas.name: Workforce & Labour
subAreas.description: Resource planning, labour allocation, time & attendance, productivity
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about workforce planning, labour allocation processes, time and attendance capture, subcontractor headcount management, and how labour productivity is measured and improved.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Labour allocation decided ad-hoc on site; time and attendance captured on paper timesheets; productivity not formally measured
benchmarks.maturity_2: Weekly labour plans prepared by foremen; basic timesheets processed manually; some productivity tracking via daily output records
benchmarks.maturity_3: Resource-loaded schedules used for labour planning; digital time and attendance system in place; productivity metrics reported weekly
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated workforce management platform with real-time attendance, biometric access, and daily productivity dashboards aligned to work packages
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven labour forecasting, wearable-enabled time tracking, and automated productivity benchmarking against industry norms with corrective action workflows

subAreas.name: Equipment & Asset Management
subAreas.description: Plant management, utilisation tracking, maintenance scheduling
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how plant and equipment is managed, tracked, and maintained, including utilisation monitoring, maintenance scheduling, hire vs own decisions, and asset lifecycle management.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Plant register maintained on paper or spreadsheets; maintenance scheduled informally; utilisation not tracked
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic asset register in place; preventive maintenance schedules created but often reactive; hire agreements managed manually
benchmarks.maturity_3: Digital plant management system with maintenance schedules, inspection records, and basic utilisation reporting
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated asset management platform with real-time utilisation tracking via telematics, automated maintenance alerts, and cost-per-hour reporting
benchmarks.maturity_5: IoT-connected fleet with predictive maintenance using sensor data, AI-optimised plant allocation across projects, and automated hire/return management

broadAreas.name: Procure-to-Pay
broadAreas.description: Procurement, subcontractor management, and payment certification

subAreas.name: Procurement & Contracts
subAreas.description: Tendering, contract award, variations, subcontractor management
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the tendering process, contract types used (NEC, FIDIC, bespoke), contract award workflows, variation and change order management, and subcontractor oversight.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Tendering and contract management handled via paper documents and email; variations tracked in spreadsheets with frequent disputes
benchmarks.maturity_2: Standard contract templates in use; basic variation register maintained; limited visibility of subcontractor commitments
benchmarks.maturity_3: Contract management system in use; variation workflow with approval trails; subcontractor scopes and obligations tracked
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated procurement and contract platform linking tenders, awards, variations, and payments; automated alerts for key obligations and expiry dates
benchmarks.maturity_5: End-to-end digital procurement with e-tendering, AI-assisted contract review, real-time variation impact modelling, and automated subcontractor compliance tracking

subAreas.name: Procurement & Sourcing
subAreas.description: Material and subcontractor sourcing, supplier qualification, RFQ
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how materials and subcontractors are sourced, the supplier qualification process, how RFQs and RFPs are managed, and how procurement decisions are documented and approved.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Sourcing decisions made informally based on personal relationships; no formal qualification process; RFQs issued by email with no tracking
benchmarks.maturity_2: Approved supplier list maintained; basic RFQ templates used; sourcing decisions documented with limited competitive benchmarking
benchmarks.maturity_3: Structured procurement process with formal supplier qualification, competitive tendering, and evaluation scorecards for significant purchases
benchmarks.maturity_4: e-Procurement platform with digital RFQ/RFP, automated bid comparison, and supplier performance integrated into sourcing decisions
benchmarks.maturity_5: Strategic category management with AI-assisted market intelligence, dynamic supplier qualification, and real-time spend analytics driving procurement strategy

subAreas.name: Purchase Order Management
subAreas.description: PO creation, approval workflows, goods receipt, change orders
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how purchase orders are created and approved, how goods and materials are received on site, how PO commitments are tracked against project budgets, and how change orders to POs are managed.

benchmarks.maturity_1: POs created manually and approved via email or paper signatures; goods receipt not formally recorded; PO commitments not linked to project budgets
benchmarks.maturity_2: PO templates used with basic approval levels; goods receipt noted on delivery dockets stored in project files; budget tracking done separately in spreadsheets
benchmarks.maturity_3: ERP or project system used for PO creation and approval workflow; goods receipt recorded digitally and matched to PO; committed costs visible in project budget
benchmarks.maturity_4: Fully integrated PO management with mobile goods receipt, automated three-way matching, and real-time budget commitment updates across all projects
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven PO automation with vendor portal integration, predictive materials delivery tracking, and automated accruals for uninvoiced deliveries

subAreas.name: Subcontractor & Vendor Management
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the subcontractor and vendor onboarding process, how compliance documents and insurances are managed, how subcontractor performance is measured, and how supplier relationships are strategically managed.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Subcontractors onboarded informally; compliance documents collected ad-hoc and stored in paper files; performance assessed subjectively
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic onboarding checklist with required documents; performance reviewed informally at project end; limited visibility of expiring insurances or certifications
benchmarks.maturity_3: Vendor management system with digital onboarding, document expiry tracking, and structured performance KPI reviews at key milestones
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated SRM platform with automated compliance alerts, real-time performance scorecards, and preferred supplier tier management
benchmarks.maturity_5: Strategic supplier relationship management with predictive risk scoring, collaborative development programmes, and AI-driven supplier segmentation

subAreas.name: Payment Certification
subAreas.description: Payment certificates, retention management, bank payments
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how subcontractor payment applications are reviewed and certified, the approval workflow for payments, how retention is calculated and managed, and how bank payments are executed and reconciled.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Subcontractor payment applications assessed manually; certificates issued on paper; retention tracked in spreadsheets; bank payments executed via manual banking portal
benchmarks.maturity_2: Standard payment application templates used; certificates raised in project system but approval is email-based; retention register maintained with some automation
benchmarks.maturity_3: Integrated payment certification workflow with digital approval chain; retention calculated automatically on each certificate; payment instructions generated from the system
benchmarks.maturity_4: Fully automated payment certification linked to progress milestones and contract terms; retention release triggered by handover events; straight-through bank payment processing
benchmarks.maturity_5: Smart contract-enabled payment automation with real-time payment status visibility for subcontractors, dynamic cash flow forecasting, and automated retention bond management

broadAreas.name: Record-to-Report
broadAreas.description: Project cost accounting through financial reporting

subAreas.name: Project Cost Accounting
subAreas.description: WBS cost tracking, cost codes, budget vs actual, CTC forecasting
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how project costs are coded and tracked against the WBS, how budget vs actual reporting is produced, how cost-to-complete forecasting is done, and how cost data flows between site and finance.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Project costs tracked in spreadsheets with manual cost code allocation; budget vs actual reported monthly from finance with significant lag
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic WBS established in ERP or project system; cost codes applied to transactions; budget vs actual available but updated infrequently
benchmarks.maturity_3: Integrated project cost accounting with WBS linked to procurement, timesheets, and site costs; monthly CTC reviews with project managers
benchmarks.maturity_4: Real-time project cost dashboards with automated actuals from all cost sources; CTC forecasting integrated into project controls; trend analysis and variance explanations
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven cost forecasting with scenario modelling, automated risk-adjusted CTC, and continuous budget vs actual reconciliation feeding live project profitability reporting

subAreas.name: Period-End Close
subAreas.description: Monthly close, WIP accounting, inter-project cost allocations
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the monthly financial close process, how work in progress is valued and accounted for, how shared costs and overheads are allocated across projects, and the timeline and pain points of the close cycle.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Month-end close takes 10+ working days; WIP calculated manually from project estimates; inter-project allocations based on arbitrary percentages
benchmarks.maturity_2: Close completed in 6-8 working days; WIP calculated from project system but reconciled manually to GL; cost allocations follow a defined methodology
benchmarks.maturity_3: Structured close checklist with assigned owners; WIP accounting semi-automated from project cost data; allocation bases reviewed and updated quarterly
benchmarks.maturity_4: Close completed in 3-4 working days with automated accruals and WIP journals; inter-project allocations calculated and posted automatically; real-time close status tracking
benchmarks.maturity_5: Continuous close capability with near-zero manual journals; AI-generated WIP estimates updated daily; fully automated overhead allocations with real-time GL impact

subAreas.name: Financial Reporting
subAreas.description: Project financial reports, group consolidation, management reporting
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about what financial reports are produced, how project-level and group-level financial reporting is structured, what tools are used, how management reporting is compiled, and how quickly reports are available after period end.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Financial reports produced manually in spreadsheets; project and group reporting compiled separately with significant reconciliation effort
benchmarks.maturity_2: Standard ERP reports used; project P&L available within 2 weeks of month end; group consolidation done in spreadsheets
benchmarks.maturity_3: BI tool connected to ERP and project system; project and group reports available within 5 working days; management pack compiled from defined templates
benchmarks.maturity_4: Self-service analytics with live project dashboards; group consolidation automated; management reports published within 2-3 working days of close
benchmarks.maturity_5: Real-time financial reporting across all projects and entities; AI-generated commentary and variance analysis; predictive financial forecasting integrated into management reporting

subAreas.name: Billing & Revenue
subAreas.description: Progress claims, invoice certification, variations, retention management
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how progress claims are prepared and submitted, the certification and approval process, how variations are included in billing, retention management, and revenue recognition practices.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Progress claims prepared manually from site records; certification process paper-based with frequent delays and disputes
benchmarks.maturity_2: Standard claim templates used; basic tracking of certified vs submitted amounts in spreadsheets; retention monitored manually
benchmarks.maturity_3: Project management system used to generate claims linked to milestones and BOQ; variation register integrated with billing; retention tracked by contract
benchmarks.maturity_4: Automated claim preparation from progress data; integrated approval workflow with client; real-time revenue recognition aligned to contract terms
benchmarks.maturity_5: Fully digital billing cycle with smart contract triggers, automated IFRS 15 revenue recognition, and AI-assisted dispute resolution and cash flow optimisation

subAreas.name: Safety & Compliance
subAreas.description: HSEQ management, incident reporting, certifications, regulatory compliance
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the health, safety, environment, and quality management systems, incident and near-miss reporting processes, safety inductions, permit-to-work systems, and how regulatory compliance is maintained.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Safety managed reactively; incident reports completed on paper; permit-to-work issued manually with limited follow-up
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic safety management plan in place; incident register maintained in spreadsheets; toolbox talks scheduled but inconsistently recorded
benchmarks.maturity_3: Formal HSEQ management system aligned to ISO 45001/14001; digital incident reporting with root cause analysis; permit-to-work managed digitally
benchmarks.maturity_4: Integrated HSEQ platform with leading and lagging indicator dashboards, automated regulatory reporting, and proactive near-miss trend analysis
benchmarks.maturity_5: Predictive safety analytics using AI and IoT sensors; real-time hazard identification via wearables and computer vision; zero-harm culture embedded across the supply chain

subAreas.name: Compliance & Controls
subAreas.description: Statutory compliance, audit readiness, internal controls
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how statutory compliance obligations are managed, the state of audit readiness, how internal controls are documented and tested, and how control deficiencies are identified and remediated.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Compliance managed reactively; limited control documentation; audit preparation is a significant manual effort each year
benchmarks.maturity_2: Key controls documented in a controls register; annual compliance calendar maintained; audit support compiled manually from multiple systems
benchmarks.maturity_3: Formal internal control framework with periodic testing; automated compliance reporting for key obligations; audit evidence centralised in a document management system
benchmarks.maturity_4: Continuous control monitoring with automated exception alerts; integrated compliance management system tracking all obligations; internal audit aligned to risk-based plan
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven compliance risk assessment; real-time control effectiveness monitoring; fully automated statutory filings; predictive audit readiness scoring
